name: ptouch-cradle
description: Two-part desktop cradle + label-catch tray for Brother PT-P750W label printer. Round 5 substantively rebuilds the tray as a closed kanban bin with four real walls (back/sides at 30 mm, front wall short at 18 mm), an interior pan-edge floor ramp curving up to the front lip, and a grabbable exterior scoop on the +Y face. The cradle drops feet (silicone aftermarket) and the cable notch (printer plug clears the 25 mm wall), and the top-edge fillet is rendered smooth at $fn=200 + 64-step stack. Quiet desk dock — function and frame, nothing more.

params.render_with_host_note: Top-level OpenSCAD parameter render_with_host (default false). When true, host_object_proxy() draws a 78x152x143 box at the printer's installed position inside the pocket. STL exports MUST keep render_with_host=false (proxy excluded from print geometry); use-state PNG renders override via --param render_with_host=true.
params.fillet_schedule_note: Two tiers + one function-driven exception. r=3 utility on top edges of cradle perimeter walls and break-edges. r=10 hero on cradle exterior corners (4x), base plate corners (4x top + 4x bottom), printer-section→shelf concave fillet on both ±X sides, AND the grab scoop on the tray exterior. r=2 continuous fillet on the BACK + SIDE walls of the tray top edge (the front wall at 18 mm wall_t=1.6 has a sharp top edge — function-driven exception documented below). NO chamfers anywhere. NO mid-tier radii.
params.tray_front_wall_top_treatment_note: Round 5: the front wall at wall_t=1.6 mm is too thin to support an r=2 quarter-arc top fillet via the slab-stack approach used on the back/sides. A bullnose (half-cylinder) cap was attempted but produced a CGAL non-manifold artifact at the corner clipping. Decision: leave the front wall top as a sharp edge. The brief's r=2 fillet rule applies on the back+side wall tops where wall_t allows it. Documented as a function-driven exception.
params.label_drop_note: PT-P750W auto-cuts tape; labels exit at z=64-79mm desk-ref, clear front wall top at z=29mm desk-ref by 35-50mm, drop forward and down into tray.
params.tape_exit_position: lower-middle of front face, confirmed from product photography
params.usb_dc_ports_face: back (above the 25 mm wall — cable runs over the top, no notch needed)
params.ramp_arc_construction: Concave circular arc connecting (y=62.6, z=1.6) to (y=92.6, z=18). Arc center on the +Y/-Z (front-bottom) side of the chord at C ≈ (84.24, -2.34) — the side OPPOSITE to the bulge. The arc bulges toward (-Y, +Z) (up-and-back into the cavity), giving a pan-edge curve: SLOPE STEEPEST AT THE BACK and SHALLOWEST AT THE FRONT. Functionally: a finger sliding across the flat back floor rises smoothly up the ramp and lifts the label over the front lip. Self-supporting in face-up print orientation (the arc's normals always point upward).
params.grab_boss_construction: Lip-thickening on the +Y exterior of the front wall, encompassing the scoop region. Built as the hull of 4 Y-axis cylinders at the rounded-rect-in-XZ corners. Boss adds 2.5 mm of material proud of the wall, giving a local thickness of 4.1 mm — enough to support a 2.86 mm-deep scoop with 1.24 mm of wall remaining at the deepest point.
params.grab_scoop_construction: Hull of two spheres of r=10 at (x=33.6, y=103.84, z=9) and (x=69.6, y=103.84, z=9) — a horizontal capsule. On the boss outer face (y=96.7), the cutter intersects in an oval indent fading from full chord (z=2..16) at x=33.6..69.6 to a point at x=26.6 and x=76.6. Reads as 'grab here' with smooth fade-out at the ends.
params.user_orientation_note: +Y = user-front (tray slides out here, printer faces user). -Y = user-back (against-wall; cable runs over the top of the 25 mm wall, no notch). Render in user-frame per id/brief.md hero_views. PRIMARY hero is the use-state render with the printer proxy installed — the 'is the cradle quiet next to the printer?' check runs on cradle-user-front-in-use.png.
params.print_orientation_cradle: Base down, walls vertical (functional/use orientation). Base plate sits flush on bed. No supports needed — all features under 45° overhang.
params.print_orientation_tray: Face up (open top up), back on bed. The interior ramp's pan-edge curve is fully self-supporting (the surface tangent always points up-and-back, never overhanging). Front wall at 18 mm is structurally fine. Exterior grab scoop is a concave indent — at depth 2.86 mm and chord 14 mm with r=10, local tangent angles stay well within the 45°-from-vertical envelope. Verify in slicer; no supports expected. The grab boss bottom face (z=0, y∈[ext_d, ext_d+2.5]) prints as a separate first-layer island (66 × 2.5 mm = 165 mm² adhesion area — fine for PLA on textured PEI).

testPrintCandidates.reason: Tray exterior (103.2 mm W × 94.2 mm D × 30 mm H) must slide smoothly into cradle slot with 0.35 mm per-side clearance. Note: round 5's tray is 5 mm taller than the cradle's 25 mm wall — the upper portion sticks above the cradle (intentional). If FDM dimensional error skews either part, tray will bind or rattle.

Round-5 substantive rebuild. The floor near the front rises along a concave arc (r=22, span y=62.6..92.6, rise z=1.6..18). Verify the arc renders as a smooth surface with no FDM faceting visible at typical viewing distance; verify the slope at the back end (steep) doesn't introduce overhang issues during printing.

Round-5 new feature. Lip-thickening on +Y exterior of front wall (66 × 16 × 2.5 mm), with a 50 × 14 × 2.86 mm concave capsule scoop carved into it. Verify the scoop reads as a 'grab here' indent (not a hole), the boss adheres cleanly to the bed at first layer, and the wall remaining at deepest point of the scoop (~1.24 mm) is structurally sound.

testPrintCandidates.feature: tray top-edge r=2 fillet on back/sides
testPrintCandidates.reason: Continuous quarter-arc roll around the tray's back + side wall tops at z=ext_h=30. Front wall top is sharp (function-driven exception per spec). Verify the back/sides fillet renders smoothly (64-step stack at $fn=200) and reads as a soft top edge in user-front-threequarter views.
Round-4 carry-over: slot side walls at 3.05 mm matching wall_thickness=3 mm. Verify the wrap reads as continuous in cradle-user-top-threequarter render.

Round-5 update: cradle $fn bumped 80→200 and slab-stack steps 8→64 so each slab is ~0.047 mm tall. The fillet should read as a true continuous quarter-circle in close-up renders, no stair-stepping visible at any zoom.
Generous corner radius on a 25 mm low wall — confirm it reads as 'quiet soft footprint' rather than 'bulbous'.
Quarter-arc concave sweep on ±X sides of the cradle at the printer-section→shelf transition. Verify the fillet reads as a deliberate sculptural move.
Tray walls at 1.6 mm (4 perimeters at 0.4 mm) are above minimum but thin. Verify wall integrity, particularly at the deepest point of the grab scoop where remaining wall is ~1.24 mm.
